messages.content:
hi there, I want you to help me to perform an Excel task, here are the brief description of each data column in the excel file, The file contains some basic accounting data needed for this project for all public U.S. companies for the 2020-2021 period. 

Definitions for all the data items:

Data Item	Definition
GVKEY	Unique firm identifier
DATE	The ending date of the fiscal year
TA	Total assets in million US dollars
COGS	Cost of goods sold in million US dollars
GW	Goodwill in million US dollars
INV	Ending inventory in million US dollars
SIC	4-digit Standard Industry Classification Code
If you understand the context, please reply " sure, please continue
Sure, please continue.

Additional information: it contain two fiscal year (2020 and 2021), some firm have different year period (e.g. their fiscal year ended at 30/4 each year) . task 1. For year 2021 only, report the summary statistics for the absolute value of goodwill on the balance sheet, including min, mean, median, max, and standard deviation) for the above nine industry sectors; task 2. Calculate and plot the mean, median, and max goodwill over total assets for the above industry sectors (in a single graph);  Please tell me the exact and detailed method to perform task 1, when I am done, I will ask about task2 and you will answer task 2 in the same way, now answer task 1 method first, thx

Sure, here's the method to perform task 1:

1. Open the Excel file containing the data and select the worksheet that contains the data for the year 2021.
2. Create a new column to calculate the absolute value of Goodwill. To do this, use the ABS function with the Goodwill column as the argument. The formula would be =ABS(GW).
3. Create a pivot table by selecting all the data in the worksheet, then go to the Insert tab and select PivotTable. In the Create PivotTable dialog box, choose to create the PivotTable in a New Worksheet and click OK.
4. In the PivotTable Fields pane, drag the SIC column to the Rows area and the absolute Goodwill column to the Values area.
5. Click on the dropdown arrow for the absolute value of Goodwill in the Values area and select "Value Field Settings".
6. In the Value Field Settings dialog box, select "Min", "Max", "Average", "Median", and "Standard Deviation" under "Summarize value field by".
7. Click OK to close the dialog box and the PivotTable will now show the summary statistics for absolute goodwill by SIC code.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any questions.
thank you but I do not know how to select the data containing information for 2021 only

No problem! Here's how you can extract the data for 2021 only:

1. Start by creating a new worksheet in the Excel file.
2. Copy and paste the header row from the original data worksheet into the first row of the new worksheet.
3. In the original data worksheet, create a new column called "Year" and populate it with the year for each row based on the DATE column (e.g. for a fiscal year end date of 12/31/2021, the year would be 2021).
4. Filter the data based on the "Year" column to show only rows with 2021 as the year.
5. Select all the filtered data and copy it.
6. Go back to the new worksheet you created and paste the data starting from the second row (since the first row contains the header).
7. Save the new worksheet with a descriptive name (e.g. "2021 Data").

Now you can perform the pivot table analysis on this new worksheet that contains data for 2021 only.
btw, there is no median option in the value field setting in PivotTable

I apologize for that mistake. You're right, the median option is not available in the value field settings of a PivotTable in Excel. Instead, you can use the following steps to add the median as a calculated field:

1. With the PivotTable containing the summarized data opened, right-click anywhere in the PivotTable and select "Show Field List".
2. In the "Field List" pane that appears, drag the "Absolute Goodwill" field into the "Values" area.
3. In the "Values" area, right-click on the "Absolute Goodwill" field and choose "Add Field (s) to Values".
4. In the "Value Field Settings" window that appears, change the "Summarize value field by" option to "None" and click "OK".
5. In the "Field List" pane, click on "Fields, Items & Sets" and select "Calculated Field".
6. In the "Insert Calculated Field" window, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name" and use the following formula to calculate the median:
`=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')` 
7. Click "OK" to create the calculated field.
8. Finally, drag the calculated field "Median Goodwill" into the "Values" area of the PivotTable. 

The PivotTable will now display the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.
When I right-click, there is no show field list

I apologize for that, it seems that the "Show Field List" option is only available in the newer versions of Excel. Here's how you can create a calculated field for median manually:

1. With the PivotTable selected, go to the "PivotTable Analyze" or "Analyze" tab in the Ribbon.
2. In the "Calculations" group, click on the "Fields, Items & Sets" dropdown, then select "More Calculations" > "Calculated Field".
3. In the "Insert Calculated Field" dialog box, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name".
4. In the "Formula" box, enter the formula: `=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')`
5. Click "Add" to add the calculated field to the PivotTable.
6. You should now see the median as a new field in the "Values" area of the PivotTable.

With this calculated field added, you can see the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code.

Please let me know if you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
where is PivotTable Analyze

The "PivotTable Analyze" tab may not be available in all versions of Excel. In some versions, it's called "Analyze" instead. If you cannot find either tab, you can also access the PivotTable tools by clicking on the PivotTable. A new tab called "PivotTable Tools" should appear in the Ribbon.
this way do not work for me, suggest another way

Sure, here's another way to add a calculated field for median:

1. With the PivotTable selected, go to the "Design" or "Options" tab of the Ribbon.
2. In the "Layout" group, click on "Grand Totals", then select "Off for Rows and Columns" (this step is necessary for the calculated field formula to work properly).
3. Click on any cell in the "Values" area of the PivotTable to select it.
4. In the "Formulas" tab of the Ribbon, click on "Insert Field".
5. In the "Insert Field" dialog box, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name".
6. In the "Formula" box, enter the formula: `=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')`
7. Press "Enter" to add the calculated field to the PivotTable.
8. You should now see the median as a new field in the "Values" area of the PivotTable.

With this calculated field added, you can see the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code. 

Please let me know if this works for you.
another simple way

You can also calculate the median for each SIC code group using a formula outside of the PivotTable. Here's how:

1. Go to the worksheet that contains the data and filter for the year 2021 only.
2. Sort the data by the SIC column.
3. Insert a new column to the right of the "Absolute Goodwill" column and name it "SIC Group Median".
4. In the first cell of the "SIC Group Median" column, use the following formula to calculate the median for the first group of SIC codes (assuming the first SIC code in the sorted data is in cell A2):
`=MEDIAN(IF(SIC=A2,'Absolute Goodwill'))`
Note: This is an array formula, so you will need to press Ctrl + Shift + Enter instead of just Enter when you're finished typing the formula.
5. Copy the formula from the first cell of the "SIC Group Median" column down to all the other cells in that column.
6. Now you can sort the data by the "SIC Group Median" column in descending order to see which SIC codes have the highest median goodwill values.

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any questions.
which std should I use, StdDev or StdDevp

The "STDEV" function calculates the standard deviation based on a sample, whereas the "STDEVP" function calculates the standard deviation based on the entire population. 

If you have the complete population data (in this case, all the public U.S. companies for the 2020-2021 period), then you should use the "STDEVP" function to calculate the standard deviation. 

However, if you only have a sample of the population data, then you should use the "STDEV" function to calculate the standard deviation.

In this case, since you're using all the public U.S. companies for the 2020-2021 period, you should use the "STDEVP" function to calculate the standard deviation.
I am using online version excel, how I add the median

To add a calculated field for median in the online version of Excel, please follow these steps:

1. Select any cell in the PivotTable.
2. In the "Analyse" tab of the Ribbon, click on "Fields, Items, & Sets", then select "Calculated Field".
3. In the "Calculated Field" dialog box, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name".
4. In the "Formula" box, enter the formula: `=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')`
5. Click "OK" to add the calculated field to the PivotTable.
6. You should now see the median as a new field in the "Values" area of the PivotTable.

With this calculated field added, you can see the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code.

Please let me know if you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
There are no anaylse

I apologize for the confusion. Here's how you can add a calculated field for median in the online version of Excel:

1. Select any cell in the PivotTable.
2. In the "Fields" pane, click the "Add Field" button, then choose "Calculated Field" from the dropdown menu.
3. In the "Calculated Field" dialog box, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name".
4. In the "Formula" box, enter the formula: `=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')`
5. Click "OK" to add the calculated field to the PivotTable.
6. You should now see the median as a new field in the "Values" area of the PivotTable.

With this calculated field added, you can see the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code.

Please let me know if you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
no  “Fields” pane

I apologize for the confusion. Here's an alternative method to add a calculated field in the online version of Excel:

1. Select any cell in the PivotTable.
2. In the "PivotTable" tab of the Ribbon, click on "Analyse", then select "Fields, Items, & Sets" and choose "Calculated Field".
3. In the "Calculated Field" dialog box that appears, enter "Median Goodwill" as the "Name".
4. In the "Formula" box, enter the formula `=MEDIAN('Absolute Goodwill')`.
5. Click on the "Add" button to add the calculated field to the PivotTable.
6. You should now see the median as a new field in the "Values" area of the PivotTable.

With this calculated field added, you can see the summary statistics for Min, Max, Average, Standard Deviation, and Median Goodwill by SIC code.

Please let me know if you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
no way to calculate the mdian

I apologize for the confusion. It seems that the "Median" calculation is not available as an option within a PivotTable in the online version of Excel.

However, there's still a way to calculate the median as a separate formula using the FILTER function. Here's how:

1. Identify a cell where you want to display the median, and enter the formula:
`=MEDIAN(FILTER('Absolute Goodwill', SIC = [SIC Code]))`

Where "Absolute Goodwill" is the name of the column containing your data for Goodwill, and [SIC Code] is a cell reference to the SIC code for the specific group you want to calculate the median for.
2. Replace [SIC Code] in the formula with the cell reference to the SIC code for the first group that you want to calculate the median for.
3. Press Enter to calculate the median for that group.
4. Copy the formula and paste it into the cells below for the other SIC code groups.

Repeat this process until you have calculated the median for each SIC group.

I hope this helps. Let me know if you have any other questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
finally, now task 2 :

Great! Here's how you can perform Task 2:

1. Start by creating a new worksheet in the Excel file.
2. Copy and paste the header row from the original data worksheet into the first row of the new worksheet.
3. In the original data worksheet, filter for the year 2021 only.
4. Select all the filtered data and copy it.
5. Go back to the new worksheet you created and paste the data starting from the second row (since the first row contains the header).
6. In the new worksheet, create a new column to calculate the Goodwill over Total Assets ratio for each company. To do this, use the following formula: `=GW/TA`.
7. Create a new column for SIC code sector names, using the table available at the following link: https://www.naics.com/search/?naicstrm=25&btnSearch=
8. Using the SIC code sector names from step 7, group the data by SIC code and calculate the Mean, Median, and Max Goodwill over Total Assets ratios for each sector.
9. Create a PivotChart with Mean, Median, and Max Goodwill over Total Assets ratios for each sector plotted on a single graph.
10. Customize the PivotChart to your needs, such as adding chart titles and changing the chart type if necessary.

I hope this helps. Let me know if you have any questions or if there's anything else I can assist you with.
